sources.title: Cross-Channel Attack
sources.author: Gordon A. Harrison
sources.notes: US Army Green Books series; primary Allied operational narrative for the cross-Channel invasion. Authoritative for US OOB, landing schedules, and corps/division-level planning.

sources.title: Normandy 1944: German Military Organization, Combat Power and Organizational Effectiveness
sources.author: Niklas Zetterling
sources.notes: Authoritative reconstruction of the German order of battle at division/regiment level; works directly from Wehrmacht/W-SS archival sources.

sources.title: BIGOT Allied tactical map series (declassified)
sources.notes: Top-secret Allied tactical maps produced for OVERLORD planning, including beach exit grids and fortification overlays. Held in multiple national archives; declassified post-war.

sources.title: Imperial War Museum archives
sources.notes: British/Commonwealth unit war diaries, photography, and after-action material — primary for UK 50th, 3rd, 6th Airborne, and Canadian 3rd.

sources.title: Mémorial de Caen — Centre Historique
sources.notes: Aggregates French, Allied, and German source material for the Battle of Normandy; particularly useful for civilian/local context and event chronology.

sources.title: US National Archives — morning reports and after-action reports
sources.notes: Primary US unit-level records: morning reports, AARs, and operational journals at battalion/regiment/division level.

sources.title: D-Day: The Battle for Normandy
sources.author: Antony Beevor
sources.notes: Narrative cross-check, not a primary source per CLAUDE.md sourcing posture. Useful for second-opinion timing and disputedBy entries.
